author: @AnotherReactor201
text:
It’s funny that her ego is exactly what keeps her from getting the funding she needs. Working for Stark Industries would give her both the resources and equipment to build her suits — and it’s a company run by a woman, powered by clean energy. She knows Shuri and has already worked with Wakandan tech before. She has plenty of options, but her ego won’t let her take them because she wouldn’t feel “iconic” or “better” if she had help. Yet that didn’t stop her from using MIT grants to build her suit, and MIT policy clearly states that anything developed with their funding or resources belongs to the university.

Riri just isn’t a well-written character.

Honestly, Morgan Stark should have been Tony’s legacy character. Riri could’ve worked better as a rival — someone who tried and failed to live up to Stark’s legacy and grew resentful of his daughter, who’s doing her best to learn from the AIs Tony left behind, with her mother guiding her in business. Morgan could start as Pepper’s assistant while still in school, with mentors like Happy teaching her self-defense and the other supporting characters helping her grow into her father’s legacy the right way. Pepper would serve as her grounding force, keeping her from inheriting the same ego that defined Tony.

replies.author: @wedgeantilles8575
replies.text:
@KingPankratos It's quite sad to see how the use of certain words change.
Another example is "neo capitalism".
It is used today if you want to describe markets, that are completly unchecked.
But that is the exact opposite of what neo capitalism actually is.
When capitalism established itself, it had no regulations, nothing. That was the "classical capitalism"
Later, some economist realized, that a completly unchecked capitalism doesn't work. You need - at least some - regulation. Best example where regulation is needed: You need at least some basic laws to protect the environment. 
These economists differt from the classical ones - because they wanted a new form of capitalism. With some checks and balances from the state.
The "neo capitalism" was born.

So the word has the EXACT OPPOSITE meaning of how the word is used today.

Fun fact: Wikipedia gets it right.
(If you want to check what I said.)

The lack of acknowledgement for Tony stark’s astoundingly resourceful success due to intelligence, mental/emotional fortitude, and resilience is crazy! They’ve tried to gaslight us into believing that Tony’s success is a product of money and white privilege? But he, as iron man 1’s villain so eloquently put it, “built this in a cave with a box of scraps. Yes Riri, I do believe that without the billions of dollars Tony Stark would still be Tony Stark! And he’d out-build you any day of the week cuz he didn’t have to steal from his university to build amazing technology. Tony stark used his brain, not his status to become a hero. What have you used to get where you are or better yet WHO have you used? 

My mother is black and she always told me to “not behave how they expect you to behave. She was referring to the ppl who have a negative opinion about black ppl. Ppl that believe we are all violent thugs, uncivilized, ratchet, overall unpleasant to be around or in public. The writers of this show just put on full display and ostensibly support the opposite behavior of what my mother told me not to do. 

Iron heart writers philosophy: • Disrespect others by stealing from them as long as they have money or work for someone with a lot of money
•Your struggle always justifies any crime
• Everyone else’s success is because of money and privilege.
•Black and brown ppl are smarter than any other race. 

My upbringing as a half black man: 
•Don’t judge others based on appearances alone.
•Don’t steal or commit crimes
•Your life, regardless of your skin color, is what you make it.
•Work hard and know that racist ppl exist but they do not hold you back from anything.
•What holds you back are your own insecurities and contempt for others. 

This show is a major disappointment as a marvel fan and an egregious message for my beautiful, brown daughters. They will NOT grow up believing they are helpless victims Disney!

replies.author: @masterpep7218
replies.text:
It's all about pure virtue signaling by a small elite, those that own Hollywood. Trying to justify their wealth by using the partly justified frustration of a minority group, pushing them forward and telling the world that Hollywood is a place of moral superiority, because this is the space where they can "give back" some of the perceived lack of power, by creating a fantasy world where the minority group does have power. And in that world senseless violence is portrayed as a freedom fight against perceived oppression, which is why all morality is thrown out the window and the world turned upside down. Anarchy. 
Any minority group, for that matter, though interestingly never Asians, because they seem to have integrated well into modern Western society and therefore are better off, thus cannot be used as a trope. 
It is the passive-aggressive type of revenge in a fantasy world, which does not work however as the stories they invent are not even relatable for the minorities that watch them. It's cool to rebel, but not to destroy without purpose. Which just proves that people cannot be fooled, not even marginalised ones: people just want a good story, not feeling sorry for themselves when trying to enjoy a piece of fiction. Except for the deranged few with far too much power in the press and due to their aggression, caused by mental derangement. The rainbow coalition will always support this insanity, because it is inherent to their nature: they are the definition of self afflicted victimhood and cannot climb out of the hole they dug for themselves. 
It also shows how little Hollywood understands people: this small cabal thinks that people are that stupid that they can easily be riled up by empty phrases. This by itself is absolutely racist, as Gary often points out. 
The good news is that Hollywood is losing: they realised far too late that their methods aren't working and are losing too much money. Better still: they still haven't understood that the cause of their failure is their distance from the general population, so they keep doubling down on their ideology, based on a feeling of false superiority.
They had it coming and it's a great watch seeing them destroy themselves, as they will have to rebuild after that and that cannot be by using the same formula. So movies will be better again, just a shame we have to be in this period of garbage for a while still, before it gets any better.

author: @justforever96
text: Still curious how the AI which is just a holographic project of a computer program, doesn't have any actual senses besides the microphone and camera on whatever device is nearby she's inside of, and can't possibly have trained herself in subtle things like reading human nonverbal cues, can possibly be "picking up sketchy energy from this guy". If you assume the AIv is literally just her friend brought magically back into existence complete with all the subconscious communication skills and intuition she learned over a couple decades in the streets, even though she's only based on the general memories of what ReeReee saw and heard her do, not an actual scan of her brain and personality, at one point she even says she "tried to analyze" the energy he radiates. Using what? The laptops built in radiation analysing machine? You have to actually detect energy in a coherent format before you can analyze the data, how does a disembodied AI do that? The part you can see doesn't actually exist, it's just an illusion. She can't use handheld tools. What did the dude just happen to be standing near a sensitive lab machine for detecting very subtle radiation emissions and she hacked into it through the net and used it to try and read him? It's a stupid line that can only work if you assume that the AI is literally just her friend recreated, and ignore the parts that literally make no sense.
